verdict: Though rhythmically engaging, the chart is severely held back by unintended three-panel hands and awkward one-foot 16th-note maneuvers during holds.

issues.problem: Placing JUMP LR while HOLD U is active creates an unintended 3-panel 'Hand' that cannot be cleanly played with feet alone on a standard single-dance pad.
Forces 16th-note transitions between two panels with one foot (e.g., U to L) while the other foot is anchoring a hold. This is extremely awkward and difficult to execute cleanly at 152 BPM.
Very tight 16th-note transitions from a double-panel jump to a single-panel hold, which breaks the physical flow of the choreography.
